bullets.text: Iran war strains U.S. politics: Senate Republicans block efforts to curb Trump’s war powers; Democrats split over Israel arms sales amid escalation.
Markets largely look past conflict: S&P 500 hits record highs, suggesting investors expect limited spillover or quicker de-escalation.
War hits energy system: Rystad estimates up to $58B of energy infrastructure damaged; Hormuz disruption risk remains a key oil/shipping shock channel.
World Bank warns disruptions can persist for months even after the Strait of Hormuz reopens, implying prolonged supply-chain and price effects.
Fed Beige Book: U.S. firms slow hiring/spending as Iran war raises uncertainty, pointing to softer near-term growth despite strong equity markets.
Trump threatens to fire Powell; push for greater Fed influence faces legal/political hurdles, raising perceived central-bank independence risk.
China growth beats expectations (~5% Q1) on exports, but domestic demand remains weaker; infrastructure spending increasingly offsets consumer pullback.
Iran war implications for China center on energy security and shipping chokepoints, potentially accelerating diversification away from Middle East supply routes.
AI backlash grows: public concern over data centers rises as big labs eye IPOs; regulators move toward requiring facilities to disclose power bills.
Amazon marketplace tension: sellers boycott ads after policy/payment changes, signaling margin compression and platform-risk for third-party e-commerce businesses.
Live Nation found to have illegally monopolized ticketing; next steps could include remedies up to structural separation, increasing regulatory risk for platform monopolies.
Corporate stress signals: Spirit Airlines reportedly nears liquidation, underscoring fragility among highly leveraged travel firms amid fuel/geopolitical volatility.
Food inflation pressure: U.S. cattle prices reach record highs ahead of grilling season, likely feeding through to consumer meat prices.
Biomed divergence: anti-amyloid Alzheimer’s drugs face renewed effectiveness debate; separately, CRISPR advances toward silencing Down syndrome’s extra chromosome.
Security risk: quantum-era threats to crypto increase urgency for quantum-safe cryptography, especially at exchanges and key-management layers.
